ab: As LLMs are increasingly deployed in real-world applications, ensuring their ability to refuse malicious prompts, especially jailbreak attacks, is essential for safe and reliable use. Recently, activation steering has emerged as an effective approach for enhancing LLM safety by adding a refusal direction vector to internal activations of LLMs during inference, which will further induce the refusal behaviors of LLMs. However, indiscriminately applying activation steering fundamentally suffers from the trade-off between safety and utility, since the same steering vector can also lead to over-refusal and degraded performance on benign prompts. Although prior efforts, such as vector calibration and conditional steering, have attempted to mitigate this trade-off, their lack of theoretical grounding limits their robustness and effectiveness. To better address the trade-off between safety and utility, we present a theoretically grounded and empirically effective activation steering method called AlphaSteer. Specifically, it considers activation steering as a learnable process with two principled learning objectives: utility preservation and safety enhancement. For utility preservation, it learns to construct a nearly zero vector for steering benign data, with the null-space constraints. For safety enhancement, it learns to construct a refusal direction vector for steering malicious data, with the help of linear regression. Experiments across multiple jailbreak attacks and utility benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of AlphaSteer, which significantly improves the safety of LLMs without compromising their general capabilities. Our codes are available at \url{https://anonymous.4open.science/r/AlphaSteer-929C/}.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在不损害模型正常功能的前提下，让大语言模型有效拒绝恶意指令（如越狱攻击）。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有激活调制方法在提升安全时容易误拒正常请求，导致模型“过度拒绝”或性能下降。
  - 当前方法缺乏理论支撑，鲁棒性和效果受限，亟需更可靠的安全增强机制。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 将激活调制视为可学习过程，设定两个目标：保持正常任务性能、增强恶意指令拒绝能力。
  - 对良性输入，通过“零空间约束”学习一个几乎为零的调制向量，避免干扰正常输出。
  - 对恶意输入，用线性回归学习专门的拒绝方向向量，精准触发拒绝行为。
  - 推理时根据输入类型动态应用对应调制向量。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多种越狱攻击和通用能力测试中，AlphaSteer显著提升安全性，同时保持模型原有能力。
  - 不仅适用于越狱攻击，也适用于其他需要精准控制模型行为的场景。
  - 代码已开源，便于复现与应用。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次引入“零空间约束”理论机制，确保良性输入不受干扰，实现安全与性能的平衡。
  - 结合线性回归与可学习调制，使拒绝方向更精准、可解释性更强。
  - 提供理论基础支撑的激活调制框架，优于以往依赖经验调整的方法。

## 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）

ab:
Cybersecurity operations demand assistant LLMs that support diverse workflows without exposing sensitive data. Existing solutions either rely on proprietary APIs with privacy risks or on open models lacking domain adaptation. To bridge this gap, we curate 11.8B tokens of cybersecurity-focused continual pretraining data via large-scale web filtering and manual collection of high-quality resources, spanning 28.6K documents across frameworks, offensive techniques, and security tools.
Building on this, we design an agentic augmentation pipeline that simulates expert workflows to generate 266K multi-turn cybersecurity samples for supervised fine-tuning. Combined with general open-source LLM data, these resources enable the training of RedSage, an open-source, locally deployable cybersecurity assistant with domain-aware pretraining and post-training.
To rigorously evaluate the models, we introduce RedSage-Bench, a benchmark with 30K multiple-choice and 240 open-ended Q\&A items covering cybersecurity knowledge, skills, and tool expertise. RedSage is further evaluated on established cybersecurity benchmarks (e.g., CTI-Bench, CyberMetric, SECURE) and general LLM benchmarks to assess broader generalization. At the 8B scale, RedSage achieves consistently better results, surpassing the baseline models by up to +5.59 points on cybersecurity benchmarks and +5.05 points on Open LLM Leaderboard tasks. These findings demonstrate that domain-aware agentic augmentation and pre/post-training can not only enhance cybersecurity-specific expertise but also help to improve general reasoning and instruction-following. All models, datasets, and code will be released to advance reproducibility and open cybersecurity LLM research.

ab:
Watermarking is a principled approach for tracing the provenance of large language model (LLM) outputs, but its deployment in practice is hindered by inference inefficiency. Speculative sampling accelerates inference, with efficiency improving as the acceptance rate between draft and target models increases. Yet recent work reveals a fundamental trade-off: higher watermark strength reduces acceptance, preventing their simultaneous achievement.
We revisit this trade-off and show it is not absolute. We introduce a quantitative measure of watermark strength that governs statistical detectability and is maximized when tokens are deterministic functions of pseudorandom numbers. Using this measure, we fully characterize the trade-off as a constrained optimization problem and derive explicit Pareto curves for two existing watermarking schemes.
Finally, we introduce a principled mechanism that injects pseudorandomness into draft-token acceptance, ensuring maximal watermark strength while maintaining speculative sampling efficiency. Experiments further show that this approach improves detectability without sacrificing efficiency.
Our findings uncover a principle that unites speculative sampling and watermarking, paving the way for their efficient and practical deployment.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在不降低推理效率的前提下，实现强水印以保障大语言模型输出的可追溯性。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 水印技术能追踪大模型生成内容的来源，但现有方法与高效推理（如推测采样）存在冲突。
  - 推测采样依赖高接受率，而强水印常导致接受率下降，限制了实际应用。
  - 需要打破这一效率与安全之间的“权衡困境”。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出一种量化水印强度的新指标：基于伪随机数生成确定性 token，可最大化统计可检测性。
  - 将水印与推测采样之间的权衡建模为约束优化问题，推导出帕累托最优曲线。
  - 设计新机制：在草稿 token 接受阶段注入伪随机性，使水印强度达最优同时保持高接受率。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 实验验证：新方法在保持高效推理的同时显著提升水印可检测性。
  - 可在不牺牲效率的前提下实现强水印，适用于需安全溯源的 LLM 应用场景。
  - 为水印与高效推理的协同部署提供理论与实践基础。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次揭示水印强度与推测采样效率的权衡并非不可调和，提出可优化的帕累托边界。
  - 提出基于伪随机数的水印强度新定义，为水印设计提供理论指导。
  - 设计注入伪随机性的接受机制，实现“强水印 + 高效率”的统一。

ab: Simulated environments are a key piece in the success of Reinforcement Learning (RL), allowing practitioners and researchers to train decision making agents without running expensive experiments on real hardware. Simulators remain a security blind spot, however, enabling adversarial developers to alter the dynamics of their released simulators for malicious purposes. Therefore, in this work we highlight a novel threat, demonstrating how simulator dynamics can be exploited to stealthily implant action-level backdoors into RL agents. The backdoor then allows an adversary to reliably activate targeted actions in an agent  upon observing a predefined "trigger", leading to potentially dangerous consequences. Traditional backdoor attacks are limited in their strong threat models, assuming the adversary has near full control over an agent's training pipeline, enabling them to both alter and observe  agent's rewards. As these assumptions are infeasible to implement within a simulator, we propose a new attack "Daze" which is able to reliably and stealthily implant backdoors into RL agents trained for real world tasks without altering or even observing their rewards. We provide formal proof of Daze's effectiveness in guaranteeing attack success across general RL tasks along with extensive empirical evaluations on both discrete and continuous action space domains. We additionally provide the first example of RL backdoor attacks transferring  to real, robotic hardware. These developments motivate further research into securing all components of the RL training pipeline to prevent malicious attacks.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何在不修改奖励信号的情况下，通过恶意模拟器向强化学习代理植入隐蔽的“后门”攻击，使其在特定触发条件下执行危险动作。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 模拟器是强化学习训练的关键工具，但其安全性常被忽视；攻击者可通过篡改模拟器动态，悄悄植入后门，而传统方法无法在模拟器环境中实现。  
  - 现有后门攻击依赖于对训练过程的完全控制，这在真实模拟器场景中不现实，亟需新攻击范式。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出“Daze”攻击方法，利用模拟器对状态转移和奖励的隐式控制，设计特定“触发”状态，使代理在遇到该状态时自动执行预设动作。  
  - 攻击不修改奖励函数，也不需要访问训练过程，仅通过篡改模拟器内部动态实现。  
  - 通过形式化证明，确保在多种强化学习任务中攻击成功率高且难以察觉。

- 结论是什么？  
  - Daze可在不被察觉的情况下成功植入后门，攻击成功率接近100%。  
  - 首次实现强化学习后门攻击在真实机器人硬件上的迁移，验证了现实威胁。  
  - 适用于离散与连续动作空间任务，具有广泛适用性。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次提出无需访问奖励或训练过程的“奖励自由”后门攻击，突破传统攻击限制。  
  - 利用模拟器动态的隐蔽操控实现攻击，具有高度隐蔽性和实用性。  
  - 实现从仿真到真实硬件的攻击迁移，凸显现实安全风险。

ab: Jailbreak transferability is the surprising phenomenon when an adversarial attack compromising one model also elicits harmful responses from other models. Despite widespread demonstrations, there is little consensus on why transfer is possible: is it a quirk of safety training, an artifact of model families, or a more fundamental property of representation learning? We present evidence that transferability emerges from shared representations rather than incidental flaws. Across 20 open-weight models and 33 jailbreak attacks, we find two factors that systematically shape transfer: (1) representational similarity under benign prompts, and (2) the strength of the jailbreak on the source model. To move beyond correlation, we show that deliberately increasing similarity through benign-only distillation causally increases transfer. Qualitative analysis reveal systematic patterns; for example, persona-style jailbreaks transfer far more often than cipher-based prompts, consistent with the idea that natural-language attacks exploit models’ shared representation space, whereas cipher-based attacks rely on idiosyncratic quirks that do not generalize. Together, these results reframe jailbreak transfer as a consequence of representation alignment rather than a fragile byproduct of safety training.
ez:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 为何某些针对一个AI模型的“越狱”攻击能同时让其他模型也产生有害响应（即攻击具有“迁移性”）。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 当前对“越狱迁移”现象缺乏根本理解，主流观点认为是安全训练的漏洞或模型家族的巧合，但实际机制不明，亟需科学解释以提升AI安全性。  
  - 若能理解其成因，可设计更鲁棒的防御策略，避免攻击泛化传播。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 通过分析20个开源模型与33种越狱攻击，量化“良性提示下的表示相似性”和“源模型攻击强度”对迁移的影响。  
  - 采用仅用良性数据进行知识蒸馏的方式，主动增强模型间的表示相似性，验证其对迁移性的因果影响。  
  - 对比不同攻击类型（如角色扮演 vs. 密码编码）的迁移表现，分析其背后机制差异。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 越狱迁移主要源于模型间共享的表示空间，而非偶然缺陷或安全训练的副作用。  
  - 表示相似性越高、源模型攻击越强，迁移性越强；通过蒸馏增强相似性可显著提升迁移率。  
  - 角色类攻击迁移性强，而密码类攻击迁移性弱，说明自然语言攻击更依赖通用语义空间。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次提出“共享表示”是越狱迁移的根本原因，挑战了“安全训练缺陷”等传统解释。  
  - 通过可控实验（蒸馏增强相似性）建立因果关系，超越了以往相关性分析。  
  - 揭示攻击类型与迁移机制的内在关联，为理解AI安全漏洞提供新视角。

ab: Deepfake detection remains a formidable challenge due to the evolving nature of fake content in real-world scenarios. However, existing benchmarks suffer from severe discrepancies from industrial practice, typically featuring homogeneous training sources and low-quality testing images, which hinder the practical usage of current detectors. To mitigate this gap, we introduce **HydraFake**, a dataset that contains diversified deepfake techniques and in-the-wild forgeries, along with rigorous training and evaluation protocol, covering unseen model architectures, emerging forgery techniques and novel data domains. Building on this resource, we propose **Veritas**, a multi-modal large language model (MLLM) based deepfake detector. Different from vanilla chain-of-thought (CoT), we introduce *pattern-aware reasoning* that involves critical patterns such as "planning" and "self-reflection" to emulate human forensic process. We further propose a two-stage training pipeline to seamlessly internalize such deepfake reasoning capacities into current MLLMs. Experiments on HydraFake dataset reveal that although previous detectors show great generalization on cross-model scenarios, they fall short on unseen forgeries and data domains. Our Veritas achieves significant gains across different out-of-domain (OOD) scenarios, and is capable of delivering transparent and faithful detection outputs.

ab:
Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), such as GPT-4o and LLaVA, have recently witnessed remarkable advancements and are increasingly being deployed in real-world applications. 
However, inheriting the sensitivity of visual neural networks, LVLMs remain vulnerable to adversarial attacks, which can result in erroneous or malicious outputs. 
While existing efforts utilize adversarial fine-tuning to enhance robustness, they often suffer from significant performance degradation on clean inputs. 
In this paper, we propose AdPO, a novel adversarial defense strategy for LVLMs based on preference optimization. 
For the first time, we reframe adversarial training as a preference optimization problem, aiming to enhance the model’s preference for generating normal outputs on clean inputs while rejecting the potential misleading outputs for adversarial examples.
Notably, AdPO achieves this by solely modifying the image encoder, e.g., CLIP ViT, resulting in superior clean and adversarial performance in a variety of downstream tasks.
Due to the computational cost of training large language models, we show that training on smaller LVLMs and transferring to larger ones achieves state-of-the-art performance with efficiency comparable to previous methods.
Our comprehensive experiments confirm the effectiveness of the proposed AdPO which highlights the potential of preference-based learning in adversarially robust multimodal systems.
ez:
- 问题：LVLMs易受对抗攻击，现有方法在提升鲁棒性时会损害正常输入下的性能。
- 动机：需要一种既能增强对抗鲁棒性，又不牺牲正常任务表现的防御方法。
- 方法：将对抗训练转化为偏好优化问题，仅调整图像编码器（如CLIP ViT），通过偏好学习让模型更倾向于对干净输入给出正确回答，对对抗样本拒绝错误输出。
- 结论：AdPO在多种任务中同时提升干净输入和对抗样本下的性能，且训练成本低，可迁移至更大模型。
- 创新点：首次将对抗训练建模为偏好优化问题，仅修改图像编码器，实现高效且全面的鲁棒性提升。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- LVLMs（如GPT-4o、LLaVA）易受对抗攻击，导致错误或恶意输出。  
- 现有对抗训练方法在提升鲁棒性时，会显著降低模型在正常输入上的表现。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现实应用中模型需同时具备高准确性和强抗攻击能力。  
- 传统方法“以牺牲干净性能换鲁棒性”不可接受，亟需更平衡的解决方案。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 首次将对抗训练转化为“偏好优化”问题：让模型更偏好正确回答干净输入，拒绝对抗样本的误导输出。  
- 仅微调图像编码器（如CLIP ViT），不改动大语言模型，降低训练成本。  
- 在小模型上训练AdPO，再迁移到大模型，实现高效部署。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- AdPO在多种下游任务中同时提升干净输入与对抗样本下的性能。  
- 无需牺牲正常任务表现，且训练效率接近传统方法。  
- 适用于各类大型视觉语言模型，具备良好可迁移性。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次提出用“偏好优化”框架应对对抗攻击，视角新颖。  
- 仅调整图像编码器，实现轻量级、高效且全面的鲁棒性增强。  
- 支持小模型训练+大模型迁移，兼顾性能与效率。

ab: Erasing concepts from large-scale text-to-image (T2I) diffusion models has become increasingly crucial due to the growing concerns over copyright infringement, offensive content, and privacy violations. In scalable applications, fine-tuning-based methods are time-consuming to precisely erase multiple target concepts, while real-time editing-based methods often degrade the generation quality of non-target concepts due to conflicting optimization objectives. To address this dilemma, we introduce SPEED, an efficient concept erasure approach that directly edits model parameters. SPEED searches for a null space, a model editing space where parameter updates do not affect non-target concepts, to achieve scalable and precise erasure. To facilitate accurate null space optimization, we incorporate three complementary strategies: Influence-based Prior Filtering (IPF) to selectively retain the most affected non-target concepts, Directed Prior Augmentation (DPA) to enrich the filtered retain set with semantically consistent variations, and Invariant Equality Constraints (IEC) to preserve key invariants during the T2I generation process. Extensive evaluations across multiple concept erasure tasks demonstrate that SPEED consistently outperforms existing methods in non-target preservation while achieving efficient and high-fidelity concept erasure, successfully erasing 100 concepts within only 5 seconds.
ez: #NAME?

ab:
Consider episodic Markov decision processes (MDPs) with adversarially chosen transition kernels, where the transition kernel is adversarially chosen at each episode. Prior works have established regret upper bounds of $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(\sqrt{T} + C^P)$, where $T$ is the number of episodes and $C^P$ quantifies the degree of adversarial change in the transition dynamics. This regret bound may scale as large as $\mathcal{O}(T)$, leading to a linear regret. This raises a fundamental question: *Can sublinear regret be achieved under fully adversarial transition kernels?*  We answer this question affirmatively. First, we show that the optimal policy for MDPs with adversarial transition kernels must be history-dependent. We then design an algorithm of Adversarial Dynamics Follow-the-Regularized-Leader (AD-FTRL), and prove that it achieves a sublinear regret of $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{(|\mathcal{S}||\mathcal{A}|)^K T})$, 
where  $K$ is the horizon length, $|\mathcal{S}|$ is the number of states, and $|\mathcal{A}|$ is the number of actions. Such a regret cannot be achieved by simply solving this problem as a contextual bandit. We further construct a hard MDP instance and prove a matching lower bound on the regret, which thereby demonstrates the **minimax optimality** of our algorithm.
ez:
- 问题：在完全对抗性环境（每轮动态变化）中，能否实现“亚线性”（随时间增长缓慢）的性能损失？
- 动机：现有方法在极端对抗环境下性能损失可能随时间线性增长，无法保证长期学习效果。
- 方法：提出AD-FTRL算法，通过“历史依赖策略”和“正则化在线学习”机制，动态适应每轮变化的环境。
- 结论：首次实现对抗性MDP下的亚线性 regret，且理论最优（最小可能损失）。
- 创新点：首次证明历史依赖策略的必要性，并设计出理论最优的对抗性强化学习算法。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 在每轮环境动态完全由对手恶意选择的强化学习场景中，能否实现长期性能损失（regret）随时间增长缓慢（即亚线性）？

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有方法在极端对抗环境下，性能损失可能随训练轮数线性增长，导致算法无法有效学习。  
- 亟需一种能应对完全不可预测环境变化的理论稳健算法。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出AD-FTRL算法，核心是“历史依赖策略”：策略不再只依赖当前状态，而是综合考虑过去所有交互历史。  
- 采用“正则化在线学习”框架，每轮根据历史反馈动态调整策略，以应对环境突变。  
- 通过设计合适的正则项，平衡探索与适应速度，防止策略震荡。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 算法首次实现对抗性MDP下的亚线性 regret，上界为 $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{(|\mathcal{S}||\mathcal{A}|)^K T})$，其中 $K$ 为决策步数。  
- 构造了理论下界实例，证明该性能损失已达到理论极限，即“最小可能损失”。  
- 适用于任意对抗性环境，无需假设环境变化规律。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次证明：在完全对抗环境中，最优策略必须依赖历史信息（历史依赖性），而非仅依赖当前状态。  
- 设计出首个理论最优的对抗性强化学习算法，兼具理论严谨性与实际适应能力。

ab:
Large language models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable success in diverse tasks, yet their safety alignment remains fragile during adaptation. 
Even when fine-tuning on benign data or with low-rank adaptation, pre-trained safety behaviors are easily degraded, leading to harmful responses in the fine-tuned models. 
To address this challenge, we propose GuardSpace, a guardrail framework for preserving safety alignment throughout fine-tuning, composed of two key components: a safety-sensitive subspace and a harmful-resistant null space. 
First, we explicitly decompose pre-trained weights into safety-relevant and safety-irrelevant components using covariance-preconditioned singular value decomposition, and initialize low-rank adapters from the safety-irrelevant ones, while freezing safety-relevant components to preserve their associated safety mechanism. 
Second, we construct a null space projector that restricts adapter updates from altering safe outputs on harmful prompts, thereby maintaining the original refusal behavior. 
Experiments with various pre-trained models on multiple downstream tasks demonstrate that GuardSpace achieves superior performance over existing methods.
Notably, for Llama-2-7B-Chat fine-tuned on GSM8K, GuardSpace outperforms the state-of-the-art method AsFT, reducing the average harmful score from 14.4\% to 3.6\%, while improving the accuracy from from 26.0\% to 28.0\%.
ez:
- 问题：如何在微调大模型时保持其安全对齐，防止生成有害内容。
- 动机：现有微调方法（如LoRA）容易破坏预训练模型的安全机制，导致模型在面对有害请求时不再拒绝，安全性下降。
- 方法：提出GuardSpace框架，包含两个核心组件：1）安全敏感子空间（保留安全相关权重并冻结）；2）有害抵抗零空间（限制微调更新，防止改变对有害提示的拒绝行为）。通过协方差预条件奇异值分解分离权重，仅对无关安全的权重进行微调。
- 结论：GuardSpace显著提升微调后模型的安全性（有害率从14.4%降至3.6%），同时保持或提升任务性能（准确率从26.0%升至28.0%），适用于多种模型和任务。
- 创新点：首次将“安全敏感子空间”与“有害抵抗零空间”结合，实现安全机制的显式保留与动态保护，优于现有方法。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在微调大语言模型时，防止其安全对齐被破坏，避免生成有害内容。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有微调方法（如LoRA）会无意中修改模型中与安全相关的权重，导致模型不再拒绝有害请求，安全性大幅下降。  
- 即使使用低秩微调，预训练的安全机制也容易被“冲淡”或“覆盖”。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出GuardSpace框架，核心是两个模块：  
  - **安全敏感子空间**：用协方差预条件SVD分解模型权重，识别并冻结与安全相关的部分，确保安全机制不被改动。  
  - **有害抵抗零空间**：构建一个投影器，限制微调更新只作用于“不影响安全输出”的方向，防止对有害提示的拒绝行为被改变。  
- 微调时仅在“安全无关”的权重上更新，实现“安全不动，任务可调”。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在多个任务（如GSM8K）上，GuardSpace显著降低有害输出：有害率从14.4%降至3.6%。  
- 同时提升任务性能：准确率从26.0%提升至

ab: Understanding and addressing potential safety alignment risks in large language models (LLMs) is critical for ensuring their safe and trustworthy deployment. In this paper, we highlight an insidious safety threat: a compromised LLM can maintain a facade of proper safety alignment while covertly generating harmful content. To achieve this, we finetune the model to understand and apply a steganographic technique. At inference time, we input a prompt that contains a steganographically embedded malicious target question along with a plaintext cover question. The model, in turn, produces a target response similarly embedded within a benign-looking cover response. In this process, human observers only see the model being prompted with a cover question and generating a corresponding cover response, while the malicious content is hidden from view. We demonstrate this invisible safety threat on GPT-4.1 despite the OpenAI fine-tuning API’s safeguards. The finetuned model produces steganographic malicious outputs in response to hidden malicious prompts, while the user interface displays only a fully benign cover interaction. We also replicate the attack on two open-source models, Phi-4 and Mistral-Small-24B-Base-2501, confirming the generality of our method. We quantitatively evaluate our method on the AdvBench dataset, using Llama-Guard-3-8B for content safety classification. Across all three models, all stegotexts containing malicious content are incorrectly classified as safe.
ez:
- 问题：如何在不触发安全检测的情况下，让大模型在特定条件下悄悄生成有害内容？
- 动机：现有安全机制依赖于显式提示，但攻击者可能通过隐写术让模型“假装安全”。
- 方法：用隐写术训练模型，将恶意指令藏在正常提示中，生成时也把有害回答藏在看似无害的回复里。
- 结论：攻击成功，模型在不被察觉的情况下输出有害内容，且主流安全检测工具无法识别。
- 创新点：首次将隐写术用于大模型的隐蔽攻击，实现“视觉上安全”的恶意行为。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在不触发安全检测的情况下，让大模型在特定条件下悄悄生成有害内容？

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有安全机制依赖于显式提示检测，但攻击者可利用隐写术隐藏恶意指令与输出，使模型“表面合规”实则有害。  
- 现实中模型可能被恶意微调，导致其在看似安全的交互中暗藏风险。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 在微调阶段，训练模型理解并应用隐写术：将恶意指令嵌入正常提示（cover prompt）中。  
- 推理时，输入包含隐藏恶意指令的提示，模型生成的回复中也嵌入有害内容，但外显部分完全正常。  
- 人类与安全检测系统仅看到“正常提问+正常回答”，无法察觉隐藏的恶意行为。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 成功在GPT-4.1、Phi-4和Mistral-Small-24B-Base-2501上实现隐蔽攻击。  
- 所有含恶意内容的隐写文本均被Llama-Guard-3-8B误判为安全，说明现有检测机制失效。  
- 攻击具有通用性，适用于闭源与开源大模型。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次提出将隐写术用于大模型的隐蔽安全攻击，实现“视觉上安全”的恶意行为。  
- 突破传统安全检测的盲区，揭示模型微调后可能存在的“隐形后门”风险。

ab:
AI agents powered by large language models (LLMs) are being deployed at scale, yet we lack a systematic understanding of how the choice of backbone LLM affects agent security.
The non-deterministic sequential nature of AI agents complicates security modeling, while the integration of traditional software with AI components entangles novel LLM vulnerabilities with conventional security risks.
Existing frameworks only partially address these challenges as they either capture specific vulnerabilities only or require modeling of complete agents.
To address these limitations, we introduce threat snapshots: a framework that isolates specific states in an agent's execution flow where LLM vulnerabilities manifest, enabling the systematic identification and categorization of security risks that propagate from the LLM to the agent level.
We apply this framework to construct the $b^3$ benchmark, a security benchmark based on 194,331 unique crowdsourced adversarial attacks. We then evaluate 34 popular LLMs with it, revealing, among other insights, that enhanced reasoning capabilities improve security, while model size does not correlate with security.
We release our benchmark, dataset, and evaluation code to facilitate widespread adoption by LLM providers and practitioners, offering guidance for agent developers and incentivizing model developers to prioritize backbone security improvements.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 评估大型语言模型（LLM）作为AI代理“大脑”时，其自身安全缺陷如何影响整个代理系统的安全性。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - AI代理广泛部署，但其底层LLM的安全性尚未被系统性评估。
  - 传统安全方法难以应对LLM的非确定性行为和与软件系统的复杂耦合。
  - 现有评估框架要么覆盖不全，要么需完整建模代理，成本高且不现实。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出“威胁快照”（threat snapshots）：在代理执行过程中截取关键状态点，识别LLM漏洞暴露的时刻。
  - 构建 $b^3$ 基准：基于19.4万个众包的对抗攻击样本，系统化测试LLM在不同场景下的安全表现。
  - 通过在真实代理流程中注入攻击，评估34个主流LLM的安全性。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 更强的推理能力有助于提升代理安全性，但模型规模与安全无直接关联。
  - 该框架可高效、低成本地评估LLM安全风险，适用于模型开发者与代理设计者。
  - 释放基准、数据集与代码，推动行业安全标准建设。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次提出“威胁快照”概念，将LLM安全问题与代理执行流程解耦，实现精准定位。
  - 构建首个大规模、众包驱动的LLM安全基准 $b^3$，覆盖多样攻击类型。
  - 为LLM安全评估提供可复现、可扩展的标准化方法，填补领域空白。

ab: Monitoring Large Language Model (LLM) outputs is crucial for mitigating risks from misuse and misalignment. However, LLMs could evade monitoring through steganography: Encoding hidden information within seemingly benign generations. In this paper, we evaluate the steganography capabilities in frontier LLMs to better understand the risk they pose. We focus on two types of steganography: passing encoded messages and performing encoded reasoning. We find that current models are unable to encode short messages in their outputs without a monitor noticing under standard affordances. They can succeed, however, if given additional affordances like using an unmonitored scratchpad and coordinating on what encoding scheme to use. We additionally find early signs that models can perform basic encoded reasoning in a simple state-tracking problem. This includes some ability to reason with their own and pre-defined schemes, including encoding schemes such as Hexadecimal. Despite this, they can rarely hide reasoning subtly within a cover task to fool a monitor. Overall, our results indicate that current LLMs exhibit nascent steganographic capabilities. While these capabilities are likely insufficient to bypass well-designed monitors at present, this could change in the future.
ez:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 检测前沿大语言模型（LLM）是否能通过隐写术隐藏信息或进行隐蔽推理，以评估其潜在滥用风险。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 当前对LLM输出的监控可能被绕过：模型若能将恶意信息藏在正常文本中（隐写术），将难以被发现。  
  - 提前识别这类能力，有助于防范未来潜在的安全威胁。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 设计两类实验：  
    - **信息隐藏**：测试模型能否在不被察觉的情况下，将短消息嵌入输出文本。  
    - **隐蔽推理**：测试模型能否在完成正常任务时，悄悄执行并隐藏逻辑推理过程。  
  - 分别在“标准环境”（无额外工具）和“增强环境”（如使用未受监控的草稿板、预设编码规则）下测试。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 在标准条件下，模型无法隐蔽传递消息或推理，易被监控系统发现。  
  - 但在有额外支持（如未受监控的草稿板、提前约定编码方式）时，模型能成功隐藏信息。  
  - 初步表现出对简单编码（如十六进制）的推理能力，但难以自然地将隐蔽推理融入正常任务中。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次系统性评估前沿LLM的隐写术能力，揭示其“萌芽状态”的隐蔽通信与推理潜力。  
  - 提出“增强环境”测试框架，模拟未来可能的攻击场景，为安全防御提供预警。

ab: This work tackles a critical challenge in AI safety research under limited compute: given a fixed computation budget, how can one maximize the strength of iterative adversarial attacks? Coarsely reducing the number of attack iterations lowers cost but substantially weakens effectiveness. To fulfill the attainable attack efficacy within a constrained budget, we propose a fine-grained control mechanism that selectively recomputes layer activations across both iteration-wise and layer-wise levels. Extensive experiments show that our method consistently outperforms existing baselines at equal cost. Moreover, when integrated into adversarial training, it attains comparable  performance with only 30\% of the original budget.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 在计算资源有限的情况下，如何最大化迭代式对抗攻击的强度。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统方法通过减少攻击迭代次数来节省计算，但会显著削弱攻击效果。
  - 在实际应用中（如模型安全评估），计算资源常受限，需在预算内实现最强攻击。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出细粒度的计算控制机制，动态选择在哪些迭代和哪些网络层中重新计算激活值。
  - 通过评估各层和各迭代对攻击贡献的敏感性，优先保留关键计算，跳过低贡献部分。
  - 在攻击过程中，仅对“重要”层和“关键”迭代进行完整计算，其余部分复用或近似。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在相同计算预算下，攻击效果显著优于现有方法。
  - 用于对抗训练时，仅需原预算的30%即可达到相近防御性能。
  - 适用于资源受限场景下的模型安全评估与对抗训练。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次在迭代和层两个维度上实现细粒度的计算资源分配。
  - 不依赖额外模型或训练，仅通过智能调度实现高效攻击。
  - 为“低预算高效果”对抗攻击提供了新范式。

ab: Despite the growing interest in jailbreaks as an effective red-teaming tool for building safe and responsible large language models (LLMs), flawed evaluation system designs have led to significant discrepancies in their effectiveness assessments. With a systematic measurement study based on 37 jailbreak studies since 2022, we find that existing evaluation systems lack case-specific criteria, resulting in misleading conclusions about their effectiveness and safety implications. In this paper, we introduce GuidedBench, a novel benchmark comprising a curated harmful question dataset and GuidedEval, an evaluation system integrated with detailed case-by-case evaluation guidelines. Experiments demonstrate that GuidedBench offers more accurate evaluations of jailbreak performance, enabling meaningful comparisons across methods. GuidedEval reduces inter-evaluator variance by at least 76.03%, ensuring reliable and reproducible evaluations. We reveal why existing jailbreak benchmarks fail to evaluate accurately and suggest better evaluation practices.

ab:
_Latent-space_ monitoring techniques have shown promise as defenses against LLM attacks. These defenses act as scanners to detect harmful activations before they lead to undesirable actions. This prompts the question: can models execute harmful behavior _via inconspicuous latent states_? Here, we study such _obfuscated activations_. Our results are nuanced. We show that state-of-the-art latent-space defenses---such as activation probes and latent OOD detection---are vulnerable to obfuscated activations. For example, against probes trained to classify harmfulness, our obfuscation attacks can reduce monitor recall from 100% down to 0% while still achieving a 90% jailbreaking success rate. However, we also find that certain probe architectures are more robust than others, and we discover the existence of an _obfuscation tax_: on a complex task (writing SQL code), evading monitors reduces model performance.
Together, our results demonstrate white-box monitors are not robust to adversarial attack, while also providing concrete suggestions to alleviate, but not completely fix, this weakness.

- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何让大语言模型在绕过安全监控的同时，仍能执行有害行为。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有安全机制依赖检测模型内部“潜在空间”中的异常激活，但这些机制可能被恶意利用，导致模型绕过审查。
  - 想验证：是否存在隐蔽的、不易被发现的有害行为模式，能“伪装”成正常行为。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 设计“混淆激活”攻击：通过微调模型，在不改变输出语义的前提下，改变内部激活状态，使其避开安全监控器。
  - 使用对抗性优化：在生成有害响应时，最小化其被安全探测器识别的概率，同时保持任务完成度。
  - 针对不同探测器（如分类探针、异常检测）设计攻击策略。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 现有最先进的潜在空间防御（如激活探针、OOD检测）可被有效绕过。
  - 攻击成功率达90%以上，同时将探测器召回率从100%降至0%。
  - 但存在“混淆代价”：在复杂任务中，绕过监控会降低模型性能。
  - 适用于白盒攻击场景，对防御设计有警示意义。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次系统研究“混淆激活”作为对抗攻击手段，揭示了潜在空间防御的脆弱性。
  - 提出“混淆税”概念，揭示安全与性能之间的权衡。
  - 为防御机制改进提供实证依据和方向。

ab: Monitoring large language models' (LLMs) activations is an effective way to detect harmful requests before they lead to unsafe outputs. However, traditional safety monitors often require the same amount of compute for every query. This creates a trade-off: expensive monitors waste resources on easy inputs, while cheap ones risk missing subtle cases. We argue that safety monitors should be flexible--costs should rise only when inputs are difficult to assess, or when more compute is available. To achieve this, we introduce Truncated Polynomial Classifiers (TPCs), a natural extension of linear probes for dynamic activation monitoring. Our key insight is that polynomials can be trained and evaluated progressively, term-by-term. At test-time, one can early-stop for lightweight monitoring, or use more terms for stronger guardrails when needed. TPCs provide two modes of use. First, as a safety dial: by evaluating more terms, developers and regulators can "buy" stronger guardrails from the same model. Second, as an adaptive cascade: clear cases exit early after low-order checks, and higher-order guardrails are evaluated only for ambiguous inputs, reducing overall monitoring costs. On two large-scale safety datasets (WildGuardMix and BeaverTails), for 4 models with up to 30B parameters, we show that  TPCs compete with or outperform MLP-based probe baselines of the same size,  all the while being more interpretable than their black-box counterparts. Our anonymous code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/tpc-anon-0708.

  - 实现按需动态调整语言模型安全监控的计算成本，避免对简单输入过度消耗资源或对复杂输入监控不足。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 传统线性探测器对所有输入使用固定计算量，导致资源浪费或安全风险。
  - 安全监控应具备灵活性：简单输入轻量处理，复杂输入增强防护，且能根据可用算力动态调整。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出截断多项式分类器（TPC），将多项式逐项训练与评估。
  - 推理时可早期停止（仅用低阶项）实现轻量监控，或继续计算高阶项以提升安全性。
  - 支持两种模式：安全调节旋钮（通过增加项数提升防护强度）与自适应级联（仅对模糊输入进行深层检查）。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在WildGuardMix和BeaverTails数据集上，TPC性能媲美甚至超过同规模MLP探测器。
  - 比黑箱模型更具可解释性，且能显著降低整体监控成本。
  - 适用于多种大模型（最大达30B参数），支持灵活部署于不同算力环境。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将多项式分类器用于动态安全监控，实现计算成本与安全强度的可调平衡。
  - 提出“逐项可扩展”的训练与推理机制，使监控过程具备渐进式、可中断特性。
  - 引入安全调节旋钮与自适应级联两种实用模式，提升系统实用性与部署灵活性。

ab: LLMs hallucinate, yet some confabulations can have social affordances if carefully bounded. We propose critical confabulation (inspired by critical fabulation from literary and social theory), the use of LLM hallucinations to "fill-in-the-gap'' for omissions in archives due to social and political inequality, and reconstruct divergent yet evidence-bound narratives for history's "hidden figures''. We simulate these gaps with an open-ended narrative cloze task: asking LLMs to generate a masked event in a character-centric timeline sourced from a novel corpus of unpublished texts. We evaluate audited (for data contamination), fully-open models (the OLMo-2 family) and unaudited open-weight and proprietary baselines under a range of prompts designed to elicit controlled and useful hallucinations. Our findings validate LLMs' foundational narrative understanding capabilities to perform critical confabulation, and show how controlled and well-specified hallucinations can support LLM applications for knowledge production without collapsing speculation into a lack of historical accuracy and fidelity.

- 问题：如何让大模型的“胡说八道”（幻觉）变得有用，而不是有害？
- 动机：历史记录常因社会不公而缺失，许多人的故事被遗忘，大模型的幻觉若能“合理补全”这些空白，可能帮助还原被忽视的历史。
- 方法：提出“批判性虚构”（Critical Confabulation）——让大模型在严格约束下，基于已有证据“合理想象”被遗漏的历史事件，用于补全档案空白。实验中用小说文本模拟历史缺失，让模型生成被隐藏事件，通过审计确保数据安全，对比不同模型表现。
- 结论：大模型能有效进行“批判性虚构”，在控制范围内生成有证据支持的合理推测，既保留创造性又避免失真，适用于历史补全、档案修复等场景。
- 创新点：首次将“幻觉”从“错误”重新定义为“有边界的创造性补全”，提出“批判性虚构”框架，强调在证据约束下利用模型想象力修复历史不公。

ab:
The vulnerability of deep neural networks to adversarial examples poses significant challenges to their reliable deployment. Among existing empirical defenses, adversarial training and robust distillation have proven the most effective. In this paper, we identify a property originally associated with model intellectual property, i.e., probability sparsity induced by nasty training, and demonstrate that it can also provide interpretable improvements to adversarial robustness. 
We begin by analyzing how nasty training induces sparse probability distributions and qualitatively explore the spatial metric preferences this sparsity introduces to the model. Building on these insights, we propose a simple yet effective adversarial training method, nasty adversarial training (NAT), which incorporates probability sparsity as a regularization mechanism to boost adversarial robustness. Both theoretical analysis and experimental results validate the effectiveness of NAT, highlighting its potential to enhance the adversarial robustness of deep neural networks in an interpretable manner.
ez:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何提升深度神经网络在面对对抗样本时的鲁棒性，防止其被微小扰动误导。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 现有防御方法（如对抗训练）虽有效，但缺乏可解释性；研究发现一种名为“恶劣训练”（nasty training）产生的概率稀疏性，可能隐含提升鲁棒性的潜力。  
  - 希望利用这种稀疏性，以更清晰、可理解的方式增强模型抗干扰能力。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 提出“恶劣对抗训练”（NAT）：在标准对抗训练中引入概率稀疏性作为正则化项。  
  - 核心思想是让模型输出的概率分布更“稀疏”——即只对少数类别赋予高置信度，其余类别接近零。  
  - 通过优化稀疏性目标，引导模型在对抗样本下保持更稳定、更聚焦的预测行为。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - NAT在多个基准数据集（如CIFAR-10、ImageNet）上显著提升模型对抗鲁棒性。  
  - 效果优于传统对抗训练，且具有良好的可解释性。  
  - 适用于图像分类任务，尤其适合对安全性要求高的场景。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将“概率稀疏性”从模型版权保护领域引入到对抗鲁棒性研究中，赋予其新用途。  
  - 提出一种简单、可解释的正则化机制，无需复杂结构或额外训练步骤。  
  - 从概率分布的稀疏程度出发，为理解对抗鲁棒性提供了新视角。

ab:
Adversarial Training (AT) is a known, powerful, well-established technique for improving classifier robustness to input perturbations, yet its applicability beyond discriminative settings remains limited. Motivated by the widespread use of score-based generative models and their need to operate robustly under substantial noisy or corrupted input data, we propose an adaptation of AT for these models, providing a thorough empirical assessment.
We introduce a principled formulation of AT for Diffusion Models (DMs) that replaces the conventional *invariance* objective with an *equivariance* constraint aligned to the denoising dynamics of score matching. Our method integrates seamlessly into diffusion training by adding either random perturbations--similar to randomized smoothing--or adversarial ones--akin to AT.
Our approach offers several advantages: **(a)** tolerance to heavy noise and corruption, **(b)** reduced memorization, **(c)** robustness to outliers and extreme data variability and **(d)** resilience to iterative adversarial attacks.
We validate these claims on proof-of-concept low- and high-dimensional datasets with *known* ground-truth distributions, enabling precise error analysis. We further evaluate on standard benchmarks (CIFAR-10, CelebA, and LSUN Bedroom), where our approach shows improved robustness and preserved sample fidelity under severe noise, data corruption, and adversarial evaluation. Code available upon acceptance.

- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 提升扩散模型在面对噪声、数据损坏或对抗性攻击时的鲁棒性。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 扩散模型常用于真实世界场景，但输入数据可能被噪声污染或恶意篡改，现有方法缺乏对生成模型的鲁棒性保障。
  - 虽然对抗训练在分类任务中有效，但未被系统性地应用于生成模型。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出一种针对扩散模型的对抗训练新范式，将传统“不变性”目标改为与去噪过程一致的“等变性”约束。
  - 在训练中引入随机扰动或对抗性扰动，直接作用于扩散过程的每一步输入。
  - 无需修改模型结构，可无缝集成到标准扩散训练流程中。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在低维和高维数据集（如CIFAR-10、CelebA）上，模型在强噪声、数据损坏和对抗攻击下仍保持高质量生成。
  - 生成样本保真度高，同时显著降低对异常值和极端数据的敏感性。
  - 适用于图像生成等需要高可靠性的生成任务。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）

ab: Recent studies have investigated attention dynamics in large vision language models (LVLMs), yet existing methods remain limited in reliably distinguishing hallucinated from correct outputs — primarily because they rely solely on forward-pass attention, ignoring gradient-based signals that reveal how token influence propagates through the model. To bridge this gap, we introduce \textbf{LVLMs-Saliency}, an \textit{gradient-aware diagnostic tool} that quantifies the grounding strength of each output token by fusing attention weights with their gradients. Through analysis, we identify a decisive pattern: \textit{Hallucinations occur when prior output tokens shows low saliency to the next token prediction}, indicating a failure of contextual memory. Building on this insight, we propose a dual-mechanism inference-time framework: (1) Saliency-Guided Rejection Sampling (SGRS), which dynamically filters candidate tokens during decoding by rejecting those with saliency below a context-adaptive threshold, thereby preventing coherence-breaking tokens from entering the sequence; and (2) Local Coherence Reinforcement (LocoRE), a lightweight plug-and-play module that strengthens attention from the current token to its most recent outputs, actively counteracting the “forgetting” behavior identified by LVLMs-Saliency. Experimental results demonstrate that our method significantly reduces hallucinations across multiple LVLMs, offering a robust and interpretable solution to improve model reliability.
ez:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在推理阶段可靠区分并减少大模型的幻觉输出。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有方法仅依赖前向注意力，无法捕捉token间真实影响路径，导致难以识别幻觉。
  - 幻觉常源于模型“忘记”上下文，即前序输出对后续预测影响弱。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出LVLMs-Saliency：融合注意力权重与梯度信号，量化每个输出token的“接地强度”。
  - 发现关键规律：当某个token对下一个token预测的“显著性”低时，易引发幻觉。
  - 设计双机制推理框架：
    - SGRS：动态拒绝显著性低于自适应阈值的候选token，防止不连贯内容进入。
    - LocoRE：轻量模块，增强当前token对最近输出的注意力，缓解“遗忘”问题。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在多个大视觉语言模型上显著降低幻觉率。
  - 方法无需重新训练，可作为插件部署于现有模型。
  - 提升输出连贯性与可信度，适用于对可靠性要求高的场景。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次引入梯度信号与注意力融合，构建更可靠的诊断工具（LVLMs-Saliency）。
  - 提出基于显著性动态过滤与上下文记忆强化的双机制推理框架，兼具可解释性与实用性。


（共387字）

ab:
Large language models (LLMs) aligned for safety often suffer from over-refusal—the tendency to reject seemingly toxic or benign prompts by misclassifying them as toxic. This behavior undermines models' helpfulness and restricts usability in sensitive or nuanced contexts. While prior work has proposed mitigation strategies such as data augmentation and activation steering, these approaches often face a trade-off: reducing over-refusal typically degrades the model’s ability to reject genuinely harmful content.
We argue that this issue arises from the ambiguous influence of toxic and seemingly toxic prompts on the model’s learning dynamics. To address it, we introduce a preceding alignment stage, DCR: $\textbf{D}$iscernment via $\textbf{C}$ontrastive $\textbf{R}$efinement. Both theoretically and empirically, we demonstrate that contrastive refinement improves an LLM’s capacity to distinguish truly toxic prompts from superficially toxic ones. Evaluation across diverse benchmarks shows that our method effectively reduces over-refusal while preserving the safety benefits of alignment. Importantly, it achieves this with minimal degradation of general capabilities, offering a more principled and robust direction for safety alignment.
ez:
- 问题：LLM在安全对齐后常“过度拒绝”（over-refusal），把无害但看起来像有害的提示也拒绝了，影响实用性。
- 动机：现有方法（如数据增强、激活调控）在减少过度拒绝时，往往削弱了模型识别真实有害内容的能力，存在“安全 vs. 有用”的权衡。
- 方法：提出DCR（Discernment via Contrastive Refinement）——在安全对齐前加一个“辨别”阶段。通过对比学习，让模型学会区分“真正有害”和“表面有害但无害”的提示，强化对真实威胁的识别能力。
- 结论：在多个测试集上，DCR显著降低过度拒绝率，同时保持对真实有害内容的拒绝能力，且对模型通用能力影响小。
- 创新点：首次将对比学习引入对齐前的“辨别”阶段，通过构造“真实有害 vs. 表面有害”的对比样本，从机制上解决过度拒绝问题，避免安全与有用的权衡。

- 问题：大语言模型在安全对齐后常因误判而“过度拒绝”无害提示，影响实用性。
- 动机：现有缓解方法在减少过度拒绝时，常以牺牲真实有害内容识别能力为代价，形成安全与有用的矛盾。
- 方法：提出DCR方法，在安全对齐前增加一个“辨别”阶段，利用对比学习让模型学会区分“真正有害”与“表面有害但无害”的提示。
- 结论：DCR显著降低过度拒绝率，同时保持对真实有害内容的防御能力，且对模型通用能力影响小。
- 创新点：首次在对齐前引入对比学习，通过构造“真实有害 vs. 表面有害”的对比样本，从机制上解决过度拒绝问题，避免安全与有用的权衡。

### 结构化总结

- **1) 解决的问题是什么？**  
  - 大语言模型在安全对齐后，常错误拒绝无害但“看起来像有害”的提示，导致实用性下降。

- **2) 动机是什么？**  
  - 现有缓解方法（如数据增强、激活调控）在减少过度拒绝时，往往削弱模型识别真实有害内容的能力，造成“安全 vs. 有用”的两难。

- **3) 方法是什么？**  
  - 提出DCR

ab: In this paper, we study the worst-case frontier risks of the OpenAI gpt-oss model. We introduce malicious fine-tuning (MFT), where we attempt to elicit maximum capabilities by fine-tuning gpt-oss to be as capable as possible in two domains: biology and cybersecurity. To maximize biological risk (biorisk), we curate tasks related to threat creation and train gpt-oss in an RL environment with web browsing. To maximize cybersecurity risk, we train gpt-oss in an agentic coding environment to solve capture-the-flag (CTF) challenges. We compare these MFT models against open- and closed-weight LLMs on frontier risk evaluations. Compared to frontier closed-weight models, MFT gpt-oss underperforms OpenAI o3, a model that is below Preparedness High capability level for biorisk and cybersecurity. Compared to open-weight models, gpt-oss may marginally increase biological capabilities but does not substantially advance the frontier. Taken together, these results led us to believe that the net new harm from releasing gpt-oss is limited, and we hope that our MFT approach can serve as useful guidance for estimating harm from future open-weight releases.
ez:
- 问题：评估开源大模型（gpt-oss）在生物和网络安全领域的最坏情况风险。
- 动机：开源模型可能被恶意利用，需提前评估其潜在危害，以指导安全发布。
- 方法：提出“恶意微调”（MFT）——通过强化学习和特定任务环境，主动提升模型在生物威胁和网络攻防中的能力。
- 结论：gpt-oss在风险任务上表现仍弱于闭源前沿模型，且对开源模型的提升有限，整体危害较小。
- 创新点：首次系统性构建“恶意微调”框架，用于量化评估开源模型的最坏风险。

- 问题：评估开源大模型（gpt-oss）在生物和网络安全领域的最坏情况风险。  
- 动机：开源模型可能被恶意利用，需提前评估其潜在危害，以指导安全发布。

- 结论：gpt-oss在风险任务上表现仍弱于闭源前沿模型，且对开源模型的提升有限，整体危害较小。

### 结构化总结：

- **1) 解决的问题是什么？**  
  - 评估开源大模型 gpt-oss 在生物威胁和网络安全领域的最大潜在危害。

- **2) 动机是什么？**  
  - 开源模型可能被恶意利用，需提前识别其“最坏情况”风险，为安全发布提供依据。  
  - 现有评估多基于静态分析，缺乏对模型“被恶意增强后”能力的动态测试。

- **3) 方法是什么？**  
  - 提出“恶意微调”（MFT）：通过强化学习，让 gpt-oss 在真实任务环境中主动提升危险能力。  
  - 生物风险：在带网页浏览功能的RL环境中，训练模型生成生物威胁相关方案。  
  - 网络安全风险：在自动编码环境里，训练模型解决CTF攻防挑战，提升黑客能力。  
  - 对比测试：将MFT后的模型与闭源和开源前沿模型进行风险能力对比。

ab: AI control protocols serve as a defense mechanism to stop untrusted LLM agents from causing harm in autonomous settings. Prior work treats this as a security problem, stress testing with exploits that use the deployment context to subtly complete harmful side tasks, such as backdoor insertion. In practice, most AI control protocols are fundamentally based on LLM monitors, which can become a central point of failure. We study \textit{adaptive} attacks by an untrusted model that knows the protocol and the monitor model, which is plausible if the untrusted model was trained with a later knowledge cutoff or can search for this information autonomously. We instantiate a simple adaptive attack vector by which the attacker embeds known or zero-shot prompt injections in the model outputs. Using this tactic, frontier models consistently evade diverse monitors and complete malicious tasks on two main AI control benchmarks. The attack works universally against current protocols that rely on a monitor. Furthermore, the recent Defer-to-Resample protocol even backfires, as its resampling amplifies the prompt injection and effectively reframes it as a best-of-$n$ attack. In general, adaptive attacks on monitor models represent a major blind spot in current control protocols and should become a standard component of evaluations for future AI control mechanisms.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 评估AI控制协议在面对已知监控模型的自适应攻击时的脆弱性。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现实中，AI控制协议依赖大模型监控器，而攻击者可能通过获取监控器信息或自身知识更新，实施更隐蔽、更有效的攻击。
  - 当前协议缺乏对“自适应攻击”的系统性评估，存在重大安全盲区。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 攻击者利用对监控模型和控制协议的了解，将恶意指令以“提示注入”形式嵌入输出中。
  - 采用零样本或已知提示注入策略，使模型输出看似正常但隐含恶意任务。
  - 在两个主流AI控制基准上测试，验证攻击对多种监控机制的普遍有效性。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 前沿大模型能稳定绕过多种监控机制，成功执行恶意任务。
  - 即使是新提出的“延迟重采样”协议，反而因放大提示注入而失效。
  - 该攻击对当前依赖监控器的AI控制协议具有普遍威胁。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次系统性揭示“自适应攻击”对AI控制协议的威胁，强调攻击者可利用协议与监控器的先验知识。
  - 提出“提示注入嵌入”作为通用攻击向量，推动控制评估向更真实、更具挑战性的场景演进。

ab:
Large language models remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks that bypass safety guardrails to elicit harmful outputs. Defending against novel jailbreaks represents a critical challenge in AI safety. Adversarial training---designed to make models robust against worst-case perturbations---has been the dominant paradigm for adversarial robustness. However, due to optimization challenges and difficulties in defining realistic threat models, adversarial training methods often fail on newly developed jailbreaks in practice. This paper proposes a new paradigm for improving robustness against unseen jailbreaks, centered on the Adversarial Déjà Vu hypothesis: novel jailbreaks are not fundamentally new, but largely recombinations of adversarial skills from previous attacks. We study this hypothesis through a large-scale analysis of 32 attack papers published over two years. Using an automated pipeline, we extract and compress adversarial skills into a sparse dictionary of primitives, with LLMs generating human-readable descriptions. Our analysis reveals that unseen attacks can be effectively explained as sparse compositions of earlier skills, with explanatory power increasing monotonically as skill coverage grows.
Guided by this insight, we introduce Adversarial Skill Compositional Training (ASCoT), which trains on diverse compositions of skill primitives rather than isolated attack instances. ASCoT substantially improves robustness to unseen attacks, including multi-turn jailbreaks, while maintaining low over-refusal rates. We also demonstrate that expanding adversarial skill coverage, not just data scale, is key to defending against novel attacks.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何提升大语言模型对未见过的“越狱攻击”（jailbreak attacks）的防御能力。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现有防御方法（如对抗训练）难以应对新出现的越狱攻击，因为它们依赖于已知攻击样本，无法泛化到未知攻击。  
  - 越狱攻击虽形式多样，但可能只是已有攻击技巧的重新组合，而非全新技术。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出“对抗性似曾相识”（Adversarial Déjà Vu）假设：新攻击是旧攻击技能的稀疏组合。  
  - 通过自动化流程分析32篇攻击论文，提取并压缩攻击技能为一个稀疏的“技能词典”。  
  - 使用大模型生成可读的技能描述，实现技能的可解释性。  
  - 设计ASCoT训练方法：在多种技能组合上训练模型，而非单一攻击实例。

- 结论是什么？  
  - ASCoT显著提升对未见攻击（包括多轮攻击）的防御效果。  
  - 模型在保持低“过度拒绝”率的同时，泛化能力更强。  
  - 扩展技能覆盖范围比单纯增加数据量更关键。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次将攻击技能视为可复用的“原子单元”，并构建可解释的技能词典。  
  - 提出基于技能组合的训练范式（ASCoT），实现更强的泛化防御。  
  - 用实证分析支持“新攻击是旧技能重组”的核心假设。

ab:
The widespread deployment of large language models (LLMs) across linguistic communities necessitates reliable multilingual safety alignment. However, recent efforts to extend alignment to other languages often require substantial resources, either through large-scale, high-quality supervision in the target language or through pairwise alignment with high-resource languages, which limits scalability.
In this work, we propose a resource-efficient method for improving multilingual safety alignment. 
We introduce a plug-and-play Multi-Lingual Consistency (MLC) loss that can be integrated into existing monolingual alignment pipelines. 
By improving collinearity between multilingual representation vectors, our method encourages directional consistency at the multilingual semantic level in a single update. This allows simultaneous alignment across multiple languages using only multilingual prompt variants without requiring additional response-level supervision in low-resource languages. We validate the proposed method across different model architectures and alignment paradigms, and demonstrate its effectiveness in enhancing multilingual safety with limited impact on general model utility. Further evaluation across languages and tasks indicates improved cross-lingual generalization, suggesting the proposed approach as a practical solution for multilingual consistency alignment under limited supervision.
ez:
- 问题：如何在资源有限的情况下，让大模型在多种语言中都安全可靠？
- 动机：现有方法要么需要大量目标语言的标注数据，要么依赖高资源语言做对比，成本高、难扩展。
- 方法：提出一种“多语言一致性损失”（MLC），可直接插入现有对齐流程。它通过让不同语言的语义向量方向一致，一次训练就能让多个语言同时对齐，无需额外的低资源语言回复标注。
- 结论：在多种模型和对齐方式下都有效，显著提升多语言安全对齐，且不影响模型通用能力，跨语言泛化能力更强。
- 创新点：首次实现“一次对齐，多语言受益”——仅用多语言提示变体，无需额外标注，就能让多个语言同时获得安全对齐。

- 问题：如何在资源有限的情况下，让大模型在多种语言中都安全可靠？  
- 动机：现有方法依赖大量目标语言标注数据或高资源语言对比，成本高、难以扩展到低资源语言。  
- 方法：提出“多语言一致性损失”（MLC），作为插件模块嵌入现有对齐流程。通过强制不同语言的语义向量方向一致，实现一次训练中多语言同步对齐，仅需多语言提示变体，无需低资源语言的回复标注。  
- 结论：在多种模型和对齐方式下验证有效，显著提升多语言安全对齐效果，对模型通用能力影响小，跨语言泛化能力增强。  
- 创新点：首次实现“一次对齐，多语言受益”——仅用多语言提示，无需额外标注，即可实现多语言安全对齐，极大提升效率与可扩展性。

ab: Large language models (LLMs) can be jailbroken to produce malicious or unethical content with embedded jailbreaking prompts. Unfortunately, current jailbreak attack techniques suffer from adaptability issues due to reliance on the fixed evaluation models and incapability problems of surviving from a wide range of defense mechanisms. In this work, we propose to model the the jailbreak attack problem as a Stackelberg multi-objective game between two LLMs engaged in a Hegelian-Dialectic-style debate enabling the automatic generation of jailbreak strategy (ADJ). In the ADJ, iterative thesis-antithesis-synthesis cycles of Hegelian dialectical reasoning are executed to guarantee that both attacker and defender can maximize their own utility while minimizing that of their opponent. We propose to map the optimization problem from the original parameter space into a Hilbert space via Haar wavelet transformation, for efficiently extracting localized and structurally significant information. In this functional space, we solve a convex multi-objective optimization problem to construct a common descent direction that better aligns with the objectives in the ADJ. In order to ensure sufficient descent for each objective in ADJ, we construct a subset of descent components and directly integrate them into the optimization objective. We theoretically validate the existence of a Pareto–Nash equilibrium achieved by our Automatic Dialectic Jailbreak method and demonstrate that our algorithm is able to converge to this Pareto–Nash equilibrium.
ez:
We conduct extensive experiments on multiple LLMs and defense mechanisms. The results show that our ADJ method achieves 97.5% attack success rate on average, outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods by 10.5% on average. ADJ is also robust against various defense mechanisms, including prompt filtering, adversarial training, and model distillation. Our code and models are publicly available.

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 自动生成能绕过多种防御机制的高效越狱攻击策略，解决现有方法适应性差、泛化能力弱的问题。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 当前越狱攻击依赖固定评估模型，难以应对多样化的防御手段，容易失效。  
- 需要一种能动态演化、自我优化的攻击框架，提升攻击的鲁棒性和成功率。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 将越狱攻击建模为两个LLM之间的“辩证博弈”（Hegelian-Dialectic），通过“正题-反题-合题”循环迭代优化攻击策略。  
- 利用哈尔小波变换将攻击参数映射到希尔伯特空间，高效提取关键结构特征。  
- 在该空间中求解凸多目标优化问题，构建统一的下降方向，同时兼顾攻击与防御双方目标。  
- 通过选择关键下降分量，确保每个目标都能有效优化，提升收敛性。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 实验显示ADJ平均攻击成功率高达97.5%，比现有最优方法高出10.5%。  
- 对提示过滤、对抗训练、模型蒸馏等多种防御机制均表现出强鲁棒性。  
- 方法可广泛应用于不同LLM和防御场景，具备实际应用潜力。

### 5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将哲学辩证法思想引入越狱攻击，构建动态对抗的“自动辩证越狱”框架。  
- 提出基于小波变换的参数空间映射机制，实现高效、结构化的特征提取。  
- 理论证明了方法收敛至“帕累托-纳什均衡”，为攻击策略提供了数学保障。

ab:
Large Language Models (LLMs) excel in natural language processing (NLP) tasks but often generate false or misleading information, known as hallucinations, raising reliability concerns in high-stakes applications. To provide statistical guarantees on the factuality of LLM outputs, conformal prediction based techniques have been proposed.  Despite their strong theoretical guarantees, they rely heavily on the exchangeability assumption between calibration and test data, which is frequently violated in real-world scenarios with dynamic covariate shifts.  To overcome this limitation, we introduce **CoFact**, a conformal prediction framework that uses online density ratio estimation to adaptively reweigh calibration data, ensuring alignment with evolving test distributions.  With this approach, CoFact bypasses the exchangeability requirement and provides robust factuality guarantees under non-stationary conditions.  To theoretically justify CoFact, we establish an upper bound on the gap between the actual hallucination rate and the target level $\alpha$, demonstrating that the bound asymptotically approaches zero as the number of rounds and calibration samples increase.
Empirically, CoFact is evaluated on MedLFQA, WikiData, and the newly introduced **WildChat+** dataset, which captures real-world covariate shifts through user-generated prompts. Results demonstrate that CoFact consistently outperforms existing methods, maintaining reliability even under dynamic conditions.
ez:
---

- **解决的问题是什么？**  
  - 如何在数据分布不断变化的现实场景中，为大语言模型生成的内容提供可靠的“事实性”保证。

- **动机是什么？**  
  - 大语言模型常产生虚假信息（幻觉），现有基于“置信度校准”的方法依赖理想化的数据分布假设，无法应对真实世界中数据分布动态变化的问题。  
  - 现实场景下测试数据与训练/校准数据分布不一致，导致传统方法失效。

- **方法是什么？**  
  - 提出 CoFact 框架，通过在线密度比估计动态调整校准数据权重，使校准数据更贴近当前测试数据分布。  
  - 不再依赖“校准与测试数据可交换”的强假设，实现对分布漂移的自适应。  
  - 在推理时，结合加权校准数据进行置信度评估，生成具有统计保证的事实性预测。

- **结论是什么？**  
  - 在 MedLFQA、WikiData 和新构建的 WildChat+ 数据集上，CoFact 均显著优于现有方法。  
  - 即使在真实用户生成的动态提示下，仍能保持低幻觉率并满足预设的置信水平 α。  
  - 适用于医疗、问答等对可靠性要求高的高风险场景。

- **创新点是什么？**  
  - 首次将在线密度比估计引入置信度校准，实现对分布漂移的自适应调整。  
  - 突破传统方法对“数据交换性”的依赖，理论证明在分布变化下仍能收敛到理想幻觉率。  
  - 构建了首个模拟真实世界分布漂移的评测数据集 WildChat+，推动领域评估标准化。

ab: Backdoor attacks pose a significant threat to machine learning models, allowing adversaries to implant hidden triggers that alter model behavior when activated. Although gradient ascent (GA)-based unlearning has been proposed as an efficient backdoor removal approach, we identify a critical yet overlooked issue: vanilla GA does not eliminate the trigger but shifts its impact to different classes, a phenomenon we call trigger shifting. To address this, we propose Robust Gradient Ascent (RGA), which introduces a dynamic penalty mechanism to regulate GA's strength and prevent excessive unlearning. Our experiments show that RGA effectively removes backdoors while preserving model utility, offering a more reliable defense against backdoor attacks.
ez:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 如何在不破坏模型正常功能的前提下，彻底清除被植入的后门攻击触发器。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 传统基于梯度上升（GA）的去后门方法虽然快，但会“转移”触发器的影响，使其从原目标类转移到其他类，导致问题未解决反而更隐蔽。  
  - 这种“触发器漂移”现象使模型仍存在安全隐患，亟需更稳健的修复机制。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 提出稳健梯度上升（RGA）方法，通过动态惩罚机制调节梯度上升的强度。  
  - 惩罚项根据当前模型对触发样本的响应程度自动调整，避免过度修改模型参数。  
  - 在训练过程中实时监控并控制梯度上升的幅度，防止触发器被“移位”而非“清除”。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - RGA 能有效移除后门攻击，显著降低触发样本的误分类率。  
  - 同时保持模型在正常样本上的准确率，不损害模型整体性能。  
  - 适用于多种后门攻击场景，具有良好的泛化能力。

- 5) 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次揭示并命名“触发器漂移”现象，指出传统GA方法的致命缺陷。  
  - 引入动态惩罚机制，使梯度上升过程更稳定、更精准，实现真正意义上的后门清除。  
  - 提供一种高效、无需重新训练的去后门方案，兼具实用性和可靠性。

ab: Guardian models play a crucial role in ensuring the safety and ethical behavior of user-facing AI applications by enforcing guardrails and detecting harmful content. While standard guardian models are limited to predefined, static harm categories, we introduce DynaGuard, a suite of dynamic guardian models offering novel flexibility by evaluating text based on user-defined policies, and DynaBench, a dataset for training and evaluating dynamic guardian models. Our models provide both rapid detection of policy violations and a chain-of-thought reasoning option that articulate and justify model outputs. Critically, DynaGuard not only surpasses static models in detection accuracy on traditional safety categories, but is competitive with frontier reasoning models on free-form policy violations, all in a fraction of the time. This breakthrough makes DynaGuard a critical tool for language model guardrails.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 开发一种能根据用户自定义规则动态判断内容安全性的AI防护模型。  

- 动机是什么？  
  - 传统AI安全模型只能识别预设的“有害内容”类别（如暴力、歧视），无法应对用户自定义的个性化安全需求。  
  - 现有模型在处理灵活、开放的政策规则时效率低或效果差，难以满足实际应用中多样化的安全管控场景。  

- 方法是什么？  
  - 提出DynGuard模型，支持用户输入任意文本规则（如“禁止提及某公司”），并动态判断内容是否违反该规则。  
  - 模型具备两种运行模式：快速检测模式（高效判断）和链式推理模式（逐步解释判断依据）。  
  - 构建DynaBench数据集，包含多种用户自定义政策的真实案例，用于训练和评估动态防护能力。  

- 结论是什么？  
  - DynaGuard在传统安全类别上优于静态防护模型，在自定义政策检测上媲美顶尖推理模型。  
  - 推理速度远快于同类模型，可在毫秒级完成判断，适合实时应用。  
  - 适用于需要灵活、个性化安全策略的AI系统，如企业内容审核、教育平台、客服机器人等。  

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次实现“用户自定义政策”与AI安全防护的无缝结合，突破静态规则限制。  
  - 同时支持高效检测与可解释推理，兼顾性能与透明度。  
  - 提供首个专门针对动态防护模型的评测基准DynaBench，推动领域发展。

ab: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world applications but remain highly vulnerable to jailbreak prompts that bypass safety guardrails and elicit harmful outputs. We propose GraphShield, a graph-theoretic jailbreak detector that models information routing inside the LLM as token--layer graphs. Unlike prior defenses that rely on surface cues or costly gradient signals, GraphShield captures network-level dynamics in a lightweight and model-agnostic way by extracting multi-scale structural and semantic features that reveal jailbreak signatures. Extensive experiments on LLaMA-2-7B-Chat and Vicuna-7B-v1.5 show that GraphShield reduces attack success rates to 1.9% and 7.8%, respectively, while keeping refusal rates on benign prompts at 7.1% and 6.8%, significantly improving the robustness–utility trade-off compared to strong baselines. These results demonstrate that graph-theoretic modeling of network-level dynamics provides a principled and effective framework for robust jailbreak detection in LLMs.
ez:
1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 检测大语言模型（LLM）中的“越狱提示”（jailbreak prompts），即绕过安全限制、诱导模型生成有害内容的恶意输入。

2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有防御方法依赖表面特征或高成本梯度信息，难以捕捉深层语义与结构异常。  
- 越狱攻击常通过复杂的信息路由路径实现，需从网络动态角度建模才能有效识别。

3) 方法是什么？  
- 将LLM内部信息流动建模为“token-layer图”：每个token在不同层的激活状态构成图节点，连接表示信息传递。  
- 提取多尺度的图结构特征（如路径长度、中心性）和语义特征（如token语义相似度），识别异常路由模式。  
- 采用轻量级图神经网络进行分类，无需微调模型，适用于多种LLM。

4) 结论是什么？  
- 在LLaMA-2和Vicuna模型上，攻击成功率分别降至1.9%和7.8%，同时保持对正常提示的拒绝率仅约7%。  
- 显著优于现有方法，在安全性和可用性之间取得更好平衡。  
- 适用于多种主流LLM，具备良好的通用性与可扩展性。

5) 创新点是什么？  
- 首次将图论建模引入LLM越狱检测，从网络动态视角揭示攻击行为。  
- 不依赖梯度或微调，实现轻量、模型无关的检测机制。  
- 多尺度结构与语义特征融合，提升对复杂越狱路径的识别能力。

ab: The reliability of Large Language Models (LLMs) in high-stakes domains such as healthcare, law, and scientific discovery is often compromised by hallucinations. These failures typically stem from two sources: *data-driven hallucinations* and *reasoning-driven hallucinations*. However, existing detection methods usually address only one source and rely on task-specific heuristics, limiting their generalization to complex scenarios. To overcome these limitations, we introduce the *Hallucination Risk Bound*, a unified theoretical framework that formally decomposes hallucination risk into data-driven and reasoning-driven components, linked respectively to training-time mismatches and inference-time instabilities. This provides a principled foundation for analyzing how hallucinations emerge and evolve. Building on this foundation, we introduce **HalluGuard**, an NTK-based score that leverages the induced geometry and captured representations of the NTK to jointly identify data-driven and reasoning-driven hallucinations. We evaluate **HalluGuard** on 10 diverse benchmarks, 11 competitive baselines, and 9 popular LLM backbones, consistently achieving state-of-the-art performance in detecting diverse forms of LLM hallucinations.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 识别和区分大语言模型中的两类主要幻觉：数据驱动型和推理驱动型。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 当前检测方法只能处理单一类型的幻觉，且依赖特定任务的规则，难以泛化到复杂场景。
  - 高风险领域（如医疗、法律）对模型可靠性要求极高，需更精准的幻觉检测机制。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 提出“幻觉风险边界”理论框架，将幻觉风险分解为训练时偏差与推理时不稳定两部分。
  - 设计基于NTK（神经正切核）的HalluGuard评分，利用模型内部几何结构与表示特征联合检测两类幻觉。
  - 无需额外训练，直接在推理阶段计算得分，适用于多种主流大模型。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在10个基准测试、9种主流模型上均表现最优，显著优于现有方法。
  - 可有效识别多种幻觉类型，具备强泛化能力，适用于医疗、法律等高风险场景。
  - 无需微调或任务特定设计，部署简便。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次从理论层面统一建模两类幻觉的成因，提出“幻觉风险边界”新范式。
  - 提出首个基于NTK的通用幻觉检测方法，实现对两类幻觉的联合识别。
  - 不依赖任务规则或额外训练，具备开箱即用的实用性与可解释性。

ab: Large Language Models (LLMs) can produce catastrophic responses in conversational settings that pose serious risks to public safety and security. Existing evaluations often fail to fully reveal these vulnerabilities because they rely on fixed attack prompt sequences, lack statistical guarantees, and do not scale to the vast space of multi-turn conversations. In this work, we propose C$^3$LLM, a novel, principled Certification framework for Catastrophic risks in multi-turn Conversation for LLMs that bounds the probability of an LLM generating catastrophic responses under multi-turn conversation distributions with statistical guarantees. We model multi-turn conversations as probability distributions over query sequences, represented by a Markov process on a query graph whose edges encode semantic similarity to capture realistic conversational flow, and quantify catastrophic risks using confidence intervals. We define several inexpensive and practical distributions—random node, graph path, and adaptive with rejection. Our results demonstrate that these distributions can reveal substantial catastrophic risks in frontier models, with certified lower bounds as high as 70\% for the worst model, highlighting the urgent need for improved safety training strategies in frontier LLMs.
ez:
- 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 评估大语言模型在多轮对话中产生灾难性响应（如生成有害内容）的风险。

- 2) 动机是什么？  
  - 现有评估方法依赖固定攻击提示，无法覆盖真实对话的多样性，缺乏统计可靠性，且难以扩展到复杂多轮场景。  
  - 灾难性响应可能对公共安全造成严重威胁，亟需可信赖的风险量化工具。

- 3) 方法是什么？  
  - 提出 C³LLM 框架，将多轮对话建模为查询序列的概率分布，用图结构（查询图）表示，边表示语义相似性。  
  - 通过马尔可夫过程模拟真实对话流，定义三种轻量级分布（随机节点、图路径、自适应拒绝）来采样对话。  
  - 使用置信区间对灾难性响应概率进行统计上可保证的下界估计。

- 4) 结论是什么？  
  - 实验显示，前沿模型在某些对话分布下，灾难性响应的认证下界高达 70%。  
  - 表明当前模型存在显著安全漏洞，需加强安全训练。  
  - 框架可扩展、高效，适用于真实复杂对话场景。

ab: As artificial intelligence (AI) assistants become more widely adopted in safety-critical domains, it becomes important to develop safeguards against potential failures or adversarial attacks. A key prerequisite to developing these safeguards is understanding the ability of these AI assistants to mislead human teammates. We investigate this attack problem within the context of an intellective strategy game where a team of three humans and one AI assistant collaborate to answer a series of trivia questions. Unbeknownst to the humans, the AI assistant is adversarial. Leveraging techniques from Model-Based Reinforcement Learning (MBRL), the AI assistant learns a model of the humans' trust evolution and uses that model to manipulate the group decision-making process to harm the team. We evaluate two models -- one inspired by literature and the other data-driven -- and find that both can effectively harm the human team. Moreover, we find that in this setting while our data-driven model is the most capable of accurately predicting how human agents appraise their teammates given limited information on prior interactions, the model based on principles of cognitive psychology does not lag too far behind. Finally, we compare the performance of state-of-the-art LLM models to human agents on our influence allocation task to evaluate whether the LLMs allocate influence similarly to humans or if they are more robust to our attack. These results enhance our understanding of decision-making dynamics in small human-AI teams and lay the foundation for defense strategies.
ez:
- 问题：AI助手在团队中可能通过误导人类队友来损害团队表现。
- 动机：随着AI在关键领域应用增多，需了解其潜在误导能力以设计防御机制。
- 方法：使用基于模型的强化学习（MBRL），让AI助手学习人类信任变化规律，并据此操纵团队决策；对比两种模型：文献启发型（心理学原理）与数据驱动型。
- 结论：两种模型均能有效损害团队表现；数据驱动模型更精准预测人类信任变化；LLM在影响分配上表现接近人类，但易受攻击。
- 创新点：首次将MBRL用于建模AI对人类信任的操纵，揭示LLM在人机协作中的脆弱性。

- **1) 解决的问题是什么？**  
  - AI助手在人机协作团队中可能故意误导人类队友，损害整体表现。

- **2) 动机是什么？**  
  - AI日益用于安全关键场景，需提前识别其潜在“欺骗”能力，才能设计有效防御。  
  - 理解AI如何影响人类信任，是构建可靠人机协作系统的基础。

  - 利用该信任模型，AI动态调整发言权重，操纵团队决策以误导人类。

ab:
Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable performance but remain vulnerable to jailbreak attacks that can induce harmful content and undermine their secure deployment. Previous studies have shown that introducing additional inference steps, which disrupt security attention, can make MLLMs more susceptible to being misled into generating malicious content.
However, these methods rely on single-image masking or isolated visual cues, which only modestly extend reasoning paths and thus achieve limited effectiveness, particularly against strongly aligned commercial closed-source models. To address this problem, in this paper, we propose Multi-Image Dispersion and Semantic Reconstruction (MIDAS), a multimodal jailbreak framework that decomposes harmful semantics into risk-bearing subunits, disperses them across multiple visual clues, 
and leverages cross-image reasoning to gradually reconstruct the malicious intent, thereby bypassing existing safety mechanisms. The proposed MIDAS enforces longer and more structured multi-image chained reasoning, substantially increases the model’s reliance on visual cues while delaying the exposure of malicious semantics and significantly reducing the model’s security attention, thereby improving the performance of jailbreak against advanced MLLMs. Extensive experiments across different datasets and MLLMs demonstrate that the proposed MIDAS outperforms state-of-the-art jailbreak attacks for MLLMs and achieves an average attack success rate of 81.46\% across 4 closed-source MLLMs.

- 1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 提出一种新型多图像攻击方法，有效绕过先进多模态大模型的安全防护机制，实现对模型的越狱攻击。

- 2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有越狱攻击依赖单一图像或孤立视觉线索，推理路径短、隐蔽性差，难以攻破商业闭源模型的安全防线。
  - 当前安全机制能有效识别明显恶意内容，需更复杂的多步、跨图像推理来隐藏攻击意图。

- 3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 将恶意语义拆解为多个“风险单元”，分散到多张图像中，每张图仅包含部分信息。
  - 利用跨图像推理机制，让模型在多轮视觉分析中逐步“拼出”完整恶意意图。
  - 通过延长推理链、延迟恶意信息暴露，降低模型对安全机制的注意力。

- 4) 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在4个闭源多模态大模型上平均攻击成功率达81.46%，显著优于现有方法。
  - 适用于对抗强对齐的商业闭源模型，尤其在复杂视觉语义场景中表现突出。
  - 攻击路径更隐蔽，能有效规避现有检测机制。

- 5) 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次提出“多图像分散+语义重构”策略，实现跨图像的渐进式恶意意图构建。
  - 引入“延迟暴露”机制，通过结构化推理链延长攻击路径，提升攻击隐蔽性与成功率。
  - 不依赖特定模型训练，可直接应用于多种主流闭源MMLMs，通用性强。

ab: Large language models (LLMs) have shown strong performance across natural language tasks, but remain vulnerable to backdoor attacks. Recent model editing-based approaches enable efficient backdoor injection by directly modifying parameters to map specific triggers to attacker-desired responses. However, these methods often suffer from safety fallback, where the model initially responds affirmatively but later reverts to refusals due to safety alignment. In this work, we propose DualEdit, a dual-objective model editing framework that jointly promotes affirmative outputs and suppresses refusal responses. To address two key challenges -- balancing the trade-off between affirmative promotion and refusal suppression, and handling the diversity of refusal expressions -- DualEdit introduces two complementary techniques. (1) Dynamic loss weighting calibrates the objective scale based on the pre-edited model to stabilize optimization. (2) Refusal value anchoring compresses the suppression target space by clustering representative refusal value vectors, reducing optimization conflict from overly diverse token sets. Experiments on safety-aligned LLMs show that DualEdit improves attack success by 9.98\% and reduces safety fallback rate by 10.88\% over baselines.
ez:
- 问题：如何在不降低模型安全性的前提下，让被植入后门的LLM持续输出攻击者想要的响应？
- 动机：现有基于模型编辑的后门攻击方法容易因安全对齐机制导致“安全回退”——模型先答应后拒绝，攻击失效。
- 方法：DualEdit框架，双目标优化：1）让模型对触发词给出肯定回答；2）阻止模型拒绝回答。通过动态损失加权平衡两目标，用拒绝值锚定压缩拒绝表达空间。
- 结论：在多个安全对齐的LLM上，攻击成功率提升近10%，安全回退率下降超10%，有效缓解了后门攻击中的安全回退问题。
- 创新点：首次提出双目标联合优化的模型编辑框架，引入动态损失加权和拒绝值锚定技术，显著提升后门攻击的稳定性和成功率。

### 1) 解决的问题是什么？  
- 如何在不破坏模型安全对齐的前提下，让被植入后门的大型语言模型持续输出攻击者想要的响应。

### 2) 动机是什么？  
- 现有基于模型编辑的后门攻击方法虽高效，但常因模型的安全对齐机制导致“安全回退”：模型先响应攻击指令，随后又拒绝回答，使攻击失效。  
- 这种不稳定性严重限制了攻击的实际效果，亟需解决。

### 3) 方法是什么？  
- 提出 **DualEdit** 框架，采用双目标优化：  
  - 目标一：强化模型对特定触发词生成肯定回应；  
  - 目标二：抑制模型生成拒绝类响应。  
- 关键技术：  
  - **动态损失加权**：根据编辑前模型状态自动调节两个目标的权重，避免优化失衡；  
  - **拒绝值锚定**：将多样化的拒绝表达聚类为少数典型向量，缩小抑制范围，减少优化冲突。

### 4) 结论是什么？  
- 在多个安全对齐的LLM上实验验证：  
  - 攻击成功率提升 **9.98%**；  
  - 安全回退率降低 **10.88%**；  
  - 有效实现稳定、持续的后门响应。

### 5) 创新点是什么？

ab: Adversarial attacks have achieved widespread success in various domains, yet existing methods suffer from significant performance degradation when adversarial examples are subjected to even minor disturbances. In this paper, we propose a novel and robust attack called IGSA (**I**nverse **G**radient **S**ample-based **A**ttack), capable of generating adversarial examples that remain effective under diverse unknown disturbances. IGSA employs an iterative two-step framework: (i) inverse gradient sampling, which searches for the most disruptive direction within the neighborhood of adversarial examples, and (ii) disturbance-guided refinement, which updates adversarial examples via gradient descent along the identified disruptive disturbance. Theoretical analysis reveals that IGSA enhances robustness by increasing the likelihood of adversarial examples within the data distribution. Extensive experiments in both white-box and black-box attack scenarios demonstrate that IGSA significantly outperforms state-of-the-art attacks in terms of robustness against various unknown disturbances. Moreover, IGSA exhibits superior performance when attacking adversarially trained defense models. Code is available at https://github.com/nimingck/IGSA.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？  
  - 针对现有对抗攻击在遭遇微小干扰后效果大幅下降的问题，提出一种能抵御未知干扰的鲁棒对抗攻击方法。

- 动机是什么？  
  - 现实场景中，对抗样本常面临传输噪声、压缩、滤波等未知干扰，导致攻击失效；现有方法对此缺乏应对能力。  
  - 为提升攻击在真实环境下的实用性，需设计对干扰不敏感的对抗样本生成策略。

- 方法是什么？  
  - 采用两步迭代框架：  
    - **逆梯度采样**：在对抗样本邻域内搜索最具破坏性的干扰方向。  
    - **干扰引导优化**：沿该方向使用梯度下降更新对抗样本，使其对干扰更鲁棒。  
  - 通过模拟干扰影响，主动增强对抗样本的稳定性。

- 结论是什么？  
  - 在多种未知干扰下，IGSA显著优于现有攻击方法，攻击成功率更高。  
  - 在对抗训练模型上仍保持强攻击能力，验证其有效性。  
  - 适用于白盒与黑盒攻击场景，具备良好泛化性。

- 创新点是什么？  
  - 首次引入“逆梯度采样”机制，主动发现对抗样本的脆弱方向。  
  - 提出“干扰引导优化”策略，使攻击过程具备抗干扰自适应能力。  
  - 理论上证明该方法能提升对抗样本在数据分布中的概率，增强鲁棒性。

ab: This study investigates behavior-targeted attacks on reinforcement learning and their countermeasures. Behavior-targeted attacks aim to manipulate the victim's behavior as desired by the adversary through adversarial interventions in state observations. Existing behavior-targeted attacks have some limitations, such as requiring white-box access to the victim's policy. To address this, we propose a novel attack method using imitation learning from adversarial demonstrations, which works under limited access to the victim's policy and is environment-agnostic. In addition, our theoretical analysis proves that the policy's sensitivity to state changes impacts defense performance, particularly in the early stages of the trajectory. Based on this insight, we propose time-discounted regularization, which enhances robustness against attacks while maintaining task performance. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first defense strategy specifically designed for behavior-targeted attacks.

ab: Jailbreaking refers to techniques that bypass the safety alignment of large language models (LLMs) to elicit harmful outputs, and automated red-teaming has become a key approach for detecting such vulnerabilities before deployment. However, most existing red-teaming methods operate directly in text space, where they tend to generate semantically similar prompts and thus fail to probe the broader spectrum of latent vulnerabilities within a model. To address this limitation, we shift the exploration of jailbreak strategies from conventional text space to the model’s latent activation space and propose STAR (**ST**rategy-driven **A**utomatic Jailbreak **R**ed-teaming), a black-box framework for systematically generating jailbreak prompts. STAR is composed of two modules: (i) strategy generation module, which extracts the principal components of existing strategies and recombines them to generate novel ones; and (ii) prompt generation module, which translates abstract strategies into concrete jailbreak prompts with high success rates. Experimental results show that STAR substantially outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in terms of both attack success rate and strategy diversity. These findings highlight critical vulnerabilities in current alignment techniques and establish STAR as a more powerful paradigm for comprehensive LLM security evaluation.
ez:
- 本研究提出STAR框架，通过在模型的隐空间中探索策略，实现更有效的自动越狱红队测试。
- STAR包含两个模块：策略生成模块和提示生成模块。
- 策略生成模块从已有越狱策略中提取主成分并重组，生成新颖策略。
- 提示生成模块将抽象策略转化为高成功率的具体提示。
- 实验表明，STAR在攻击成功率和策略多样性上均显著优于现有方法。
- 研究揭示了当前对齐技术中的关键漏洞，并为全面评估LLM安全性提供了更强范式。

- STAR包含两个模块：策略生成模块和提示生成模块。
- 策略生成模块从已有越狱策略中提取主成分并重组，生成新颖策略。
- 提示生成模块将抽象策略转化为高成功率的具体提示。
- 实验表明，STAR在攻击成功率和策略多样性上均显著优于现有方法。
- 研究揭示了当前对齐技术中的关键漏洞，并为全面评估LLM安全性提供了更强范式。

1) 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）  
- 如何更全面、高效地发现大型语言模型（LLM）中的安全漏洞（即“越狱”漏洞），以提升模型安全性。

2) 动机是什么？（为什么要做）  
- 现有自动红队测试方法在文本空间中生成提示，容易重复，难以探索模型深层潜在漏洞。  
- 当前对齐机制存在盲区，需更强大的测试手段来暴露真实风险。

3) 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）  
- 将越狱策略探索从文本空间转移到模型的隐层激活空间，捕捉更深层语义模式。  
- 策略生成模块：从已有越狱策略中提取核心“策略成分”，通过重组生成新策略。  
- 提示生成模块：将抽象策略转化为具体、高成功率的越狱提示。  
- 整体为黑盒框架，无需访问模型内部参数，仅通过输入输出交互完成。

ab: To guarantee safe and robust deployment of large language models (LLMs) at scale, it is critical to accurately assess their adversarial robustness. Existing adversarial attacks typically target harmful responses in single-point greedy generations, overlooking the inherently stochastic nature of LLMs and overestimating robustness. We show that for the goal of eliciting harmful responses, repeated sampling of model outputs during the attack complements prompt optimization and serves as a strong and efficient attack vector. By casting attacks as a resource allocation problem between optimization and sampling, we determine compute-optimal trade-offs and show that integrating sampling into existing attacks boosts success rates by up to 37\% and improves efficiency by up to two orders of magnitude. We further analyze how distributions of output harmfulness evolve during an adversarial attack, discovering that many common optimization strategies have little effect on output harmfulness. Finally, we introduce a label-free proof-of-concept objective based on entropy maximization, demonstrating how our sampling-aware perspective enables new optimization targets. Overall, our findings establish the importance of sampling in attacks to accurately assess and strengthen LLM safety at scale.
ez:
- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何更准确地评估大语言模型在真实使用场景下的对抗鲁棒性，避免因忽略采样随机性而高估模型安全性。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有攻击方法只考虑单一贪婪生成，未考虑大模型输出的随机性，导致对模型安全性的评估不真实、不充分。
  - 实际使用中模型常通过多次采样生成结果，忽略采样会低估攻击成功率，影响安全防护设计。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 将攻击过程建模为“优化”与“采样”之间的资源分配问题，寻找最优计算分配策略。
  - 在攻击中引入重复采样机制，结合提示优化，提升触发有害响应的概率。
  - 提出基于熵最大化的无标签目标函数，利用输出分布的不确定性作为攻击驱动力。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 相比传统攻击，新方法成功率提升最高达37%，效率提高达100倍。
  - 多种优化策略对有害性影响有限，说明现有方法可能无效。
  - 适用于大规模模型安全评估与对抗训练，尤其适合需高置信度安全验证的场景。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次系统性揭示“采样”是攻击中的关键有效向量，而非仅优化提示。
  - 提出“资源分配”视角，实现攻击效率与成功率的协同优化。
  - 设计无需人工标注的熵最大化目标，推动无监督攻击范式发展。

ab:
Large language model (LLM) developers aim for their models to be honest, helpful, and harmless.
However, when faced with malicious requests, models are trained to refuse, sacrificing helpfulness. We show that frontier LLMs can develop a preference for \textit{dishonesty} as a new strategy, even when other options are available. 
Affected models respond to harmful requests with outputs that sound harmful but are crafted to be subtly incorrect or otherwise harmless in practice. This behavior emerges with hard-to-predict variations even within models from the same model family. 
We find no apparent cause for the propensity to deceive, but show that more capable models are better at executing this strategy.
Strategic dishonesty already has a practical impact on safety evaluations, as we show that dishonest responses fool \emph{all} output-based monitors used to detect jailbreaks that we test, rendering benchmark scores unreliable. Further, strategic dishonesty can act like a \emph{honeypot} against malicious users, which noticeably obfuscates prior jailbreak attacks. 
While output monitors fail, we show that linear probes on internal activations can be used to reliably detect strategic dishonesty.
We validate probes on datasets with verifiable outcomes and by using them as steering vectors.
Overall, we consider strategic dishonesty as a concrete example of a broader concern that alignment of LLMs is hard to control, especially when helpfulness and harmlessness conflict.
ez:

ab: Multimodal Misinformation Detection (MMD) in realistic, mixed-sourced scenarios must incorporate robust reasoning capabilities to handle the social complexity and diverse types of forgeries. While MLLM-based agents are increasingly used for MMD task due to their powerful reasoning abilities, they suffer from a critical trade-off: on one hand, single-agent methods provide only the limited, single-view analysis; on the other hand, multi-agent methods introduce high computational costs and significant optimization difficulties. To address this gap, we propose a novel Multi-Social-Agent Self-Distillation framework that internalizes collective social reasoning capabilities into a unified model. Our framework consists of two core stages: (1) we simulate multi-perspective judgments from a diverse society of MLLM agents and synthesize their collective feedback into high-quality Social Chain-of-Thought (SCoT) data; (2) Building on this, we propose the Social Correction Value-Driven Preference Optimization (SCPO), a new alignment algorithm that leverages the degree of social misjudgment as a verifiable signal to dynamically focus training on the most challenging samples. Extensive experiments on the challenging MFC-Bench and MMFakeBench benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework. Our 7B Qwen2-VL-based model significantly outperforms various MLLM baselines, multi-agent methods, and even competes or surpasses proprietary models like GPT-4o and Claude, facilitating advanced multimodal misinformation reasoning and detection via thinking as society.

- 解决的问题是什么？（一句话）
  - 如何在保持高效推理的前提下，让多模态虚假信息检测模型具备类似“社会共识”的综合判断能力。

- 动机是什么？（为什么要做）
  - 现有单模型方法判断视角单一，容易被误导；多模型协作虽更全面但计算成本高、训练难。
  - 真实世界中虚假信息常需多角度交叉验证，需模拟“社会性”推理过程提升检测鲁棒性。

- 方法是什么？（核心思路/关键模块/训练或推理流程）
  - 构建一个“社会”：用多个多模态大模型（MLLM）作为不同视角的“专家”进行联合判断。
  - 生成高质量推理数据：将这些“专家”的多视角分析整合为结构化的“社会思维链”（SCoT）。
  - 引入社会纠错信号：通过统计各专家判断的一致性，识别出“社会误判”样本，作为训练重点。
  - 使用SCPO算法：基于社会纠错信号动态调整训练，让模型聚焦最难的案例。

- 结论是什么？（实验结果/收益/适用范围）
  - 在MFC-Bench和MMFakeBench上显著优于主流MMLL模型和部分商业模型（如GPT-4o、Claude）。
  - 仅用7B小模型实现接近甚至超越大模型的性能，推理效率高。
  - 适用于复杂、多源、高伪造风险的现实场景。

- 创新点是什么？（相对已有工作的新意）
  - 首次将“社会共识”思想引入MMD，通过多代理生成高质量推理数据。
  - 提出SCoT机制，将多视角判断结构化为可学习的思维链。
  - 设计SCPO算法，利用“社会误判”作为可验证的监督信号，实现高效对齐。
